So this removes the tests from the CQ in the interest of avoiding false rejects.

[views-ax] Refactor tooltip text to be cached across Views (8)
This CL continues the refactor for caching the tooltip text across Views.
This CL refactors BookmarkButton. After some investigation, and judging by the existing tests for the tooltip text of this view which have been around for a while, it seems to me like the point passed in to GetTooltipText is not expected to be something other than gfx::Point(). As a result, this CL updates the tooltip text with a max width based on such a point. Then the cached tooltip text is updated whenever SetText is called and also when the max width for the tooltip could potentially change.
From previous CL description and tracking bug info:
The tooltip text is used for accessible descriptions of Views. As a result, this is needed for the ViewsAX project, this is because this project involves caching accessible properties of Views rather than computing them "on the fly", and currently the tooltip text is not cached across all Views. Some Views do cache it locally (in those classes) but others just compute it on the fly.
Due to the large number of Views and files involved, my aim is to complete this refactor iteratively across multiple CLs. This will make it easy for reviewers but also for me. In order for this to be possible, and to keep the View builder and the compiler happy, the refactor will be split into two separate parts, the implementation and the cleanup.
For the implementation, we will have to use "clever" naming so that CLs can be largely independent and we keep compatibility with the View builder. As such, for now we will have `SetCachedTooltipText(string)` and `GetCachedTooltipText()` as the main entryways to get/set this new cached member (`cached_tooltip_text_`) on View. The goal will be to eventually rename these to `SetTooltipText(string)`, `GetCachedTooltipText()`, and `tooltip_text_`, or something similar. However, for now, because some Views have their own version of `SetTooltipText(string)`, `GetTooltipText()` and the existing `GetTooltipText(point)`, we will use the `Cached` part in the naming to keep these separate. During the refactor, we will make these latter ones simply "point" towards the new ones that affect the new cached tooltip text on View.
After all the instances of `GetTooltipText(point)` are refactored in this manner, I will go back and perform cleanup where it makes sense. Among possible action items for the clean up will be:
* Rename to just `SetTooltipText(string)`, `GetTooltipText()` and `tooltip_text_`. * Remove `GetTooltipText(point)` * Remove or rename local instances of `GetTooltipText()` i.e. `Label::GetTooltipText()` etc and if possible use only the new one on View.
Some views that currently cache their tooltip text to some degree, such as Label and ToolbarButton, will need to still keep a cached "custom" version of the tooltip text, separate from the one cached on View. This is due to the fact that these Views have custom logic for the tooltip text that we would like to keep.
This CL is part of the ViewsAX project: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ku7HOyDsiZem1yaV6ccZ-tz3lO2XR2NEcm8HjR6d-VY/edit#heading=h.ke1u3utej413

graphite: Don't try to present after failed draw
With graphite we get feedback from skia if render pass recording fails to be inserted. When insertRecording() fails the draw to update the render pass never happens. Presenting after the failed draw includes pixels that are wrong, aka it's a graphical glitch.
If insertRecording() fails for the root or offscreen render passes then skip presentation. This reuses the existing SWAP_SKIPPED path that Chrome OS uses.

AVFoundationCapture: Parse color space from buffer
The color space varies depending on the capture device. Instead of using a hardcoded color space, attempt to parse the color space from the buffer. If parsing fails, fall back to the hardcoded color space.
To avoid potential log spam, color space parsing is only performed for the first frame.
A UMA histogram has been added to track color space parsing failures.
Bug: 382451539 Change-Id: I530f36e941fcd9637ef3b38cafd4b58238aa1c31 Reviewed-on: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/6081717 Reviewed-by: Henrik Boström <hbos@chromium.org> Commit-Queue: Johannes Kron <kron@chromium.org> Cr-Commit-Position: refs/heads/main@{#1394386}

[views-ax] GetAccessibleNodeData clean up
This CL gets rid of some View::GetAccessibleNodeData overrides in unit tests.
One of the changes included changing the role being set in a test class since the role was one that shouldn't be set on Views.
Only a few remaining and then we'll get rid of this function on View.
This CL is part of the ViewsAX project: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Ku7HOyDsiZem1yaV6ccZ-tz3lO2XR2NEcm8HjR6d-VY/edit#heading=h.ke1u3utej413

coral: consecutively launching groups
Previously, we activated the desk and exited Overview after launching a group so every time when launching a group we could assume the group contents are from currently active desk. However, we now stay in Overview after launching a group, the current active desk is switched to newly created desk instead. This makes moving the second group's content from the source desk not working.
To solve the issue, we make BirchCoralProvider cache the source desk on groups generation.
Manual Test: https://screenshot.googleplex.com/3txsPjYHtiMW9dX
Test: CoralBrowserTest.ConsecutiveLaunchGroups/MergeDeskRemoveAllChips Bug: b:382326692 Change-Id: I4509e407559e5a64540dbca42088d7b150fda20d Reviewed-on: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/6076603 Auto-Submit: Xiaodan Zhu <zxdan@chromium.org> Reviewed-by: Xiaoqian Dai <xdai@chromium.org> Commit-Queue: Xiaodan Zhu <zxdan@chromium.org> Cr-Commit-Position: refs/heads/main@{#1394381}

[messaging] Only query for dirty tabs for tab grid message
Before this CL, we query for all data when looking for changes to a tab group, by looking for currently dirty persistent messages.
This ends up including both DIRTY_TAB and CHIP type of messages for each dirty tab, which leads to double-counting of every tab.
This CL specifies that we only care about the DIRTY_TAB (dirty blue dot) for each tab, making the count correct.
When the original CL for this functionality landed, the backend was not possible to use for testing, so we were not sure how the query should work: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/5909992
R=skym@chromium.org

for webui's with webviews, change media permissions
media permission requests in webviews must be approved by the embedder. Approved requests are currently forwarded without modification -- they will appear to come from the embedded origin. This CL changes this behavior for chrome: origins that embed webviews: the forwarded permission request will appear as coming from the chrome: origin.
Note that media permission differs from location permission, which is already forwarded as coming from the embedder origin. This CL does not change behavior on chrome-extension embedders, so is unlikely to materially affect any existing code or features.
This change is to support chrome://glic
Bug: 377975471 Change-Id: Ia67d4a6435ce61b46579709052af39760691c018 Reviewed-on: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/6072787 Commit-Queue: Dan H <harringtond@chromium.org> Reviewed-by: Kevin McNee <mcnee@chromium.org> Cr-Commit-Position: refs/heads/main@{#1394350}

[PM] Add OnBeforeFooNodeAdded methods to observers.
The new method is called before nodes are added to the graph, so the public interface will return null for all outgoing edges. Edges that are assigned in the constructor, so they're constant for the life of the node, are exposed to the observer an `pending_foo_node` parameters.
To implement the new semantic. splits the internal NodeBase::OnJoiningGraph() method into 2 stages:
* OnInitializingProperties sets all properties on the node that don't affect the graph topology. It's called before sending OnBeforeFooNodeAdded notifations. * OnInitializingEdges lets the node create connections to other nodes. It's called between sending OnBeforeFooNodeAdded notifications and OnFooNodeAdded notifications.
Since the original OnFooNodeAdded methods are called after both of these stages, its behaviour doesn't change, and no existing observers need updating.
R=pmonette

[Protected Audience] Network Isolation for cached KVv2 [3/3]
This CL makes TrustedSignalsCacheImpl pass nonces to the TrustedSignalsFetcher. The nonce is used by the fetcher to partition network state, to reduce information leakage when requesting trusted signals.
Nonces are UnguessableTokens generated for each combination of signals URL, signals type, and script owner the cache sees. UnguessableTokens can't be generated from hashes, so instead, random ones are generated and then stored in an LRU cache. In the case of eviction, a new nonce will be generated for the same values, which will effectively create a new network partition.
This is the last CL in a chain to partition KVv2 trusted signals network fetches using IsolationInfos based on top frame origin and a nonce unique to the <script origin, worklet type, signals URL>, which will better partition those fetches.
Bug: 379844661 Change-Id: I0ce72333868f0d54d8faa5d19d3fd16d8045e596 Reviewed-on: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/6071702 Reviewed-by: Maks Orlovich <morlovich@chromium.org> Commit-Queue: mmenke <mmenke@chromium.org> Cr-Commit-Position: refs/heads/main@{#1394342}

Use private_aggregation_promise_test() in fenced-frame WPTs
It's a good idea to use `private_aggregation_promise_test()` because it automatically resets the reporting endpoints before running the provided test callback.
It's entirely possible that this change is a no-op. Polling for reports is a destructive operation on the server, so if every test polled for reports at the end, then explicitly clearing the stash would never be necessary.

BufferQueue handle SWAP_SKIPPED
When the GPU thread decides to skip presentation it sends SWAP_SKIPPED back to the viz thread. viz:BufferQueue didn't see the SwapResult value and would still treat it as a successful presentation. BufferQueue would take the primary plane buffer that is still being displayed, due to in flight buffer being skipped, and return it to the available pool. SkiaOutputDeviceBufferQueue would not end the overlay access on real displayed buffer so when it's reused the next write access would fail. Instead, return the in flight buffer that wasn't presented to the available pool and leave displayed buffer alone.
This doesn't appear to have caused problems on Chrome OS. However, a follow up is going to reuse SWAP_SKIPPED for when render pass recording insertion fails and I observed this problem there.
Also clear out SkiaOutputSurfaceImplOnGpu::overlays_ for skipped swap. The list would just accumulate each skipped swap until a successful swap.
